claim_boundary: Pilot success/failure criteria measure pilot usefulness and boundary fit only. They do not validate production performance, certify compliance, determine truth, create legal/financial conclusions, or authorize automated action.

failure_or_stop_criteria.description: Pilot cannot proceed if buyer requires review of model weights, source code, private prompts, hidden activations, private chain-of-thought, or internal architecture.
Pilot cannot proceed if buyer cannot provide observable exported traces.
Pilot should stop or park if no buyer-controlled human adjudication path exists.
Pilot should stop or be rescoped if buyer asks for production validation, automated verdicts, compliance certification, or truth validation.
Pilot should pause if procurement, contract terms, paid pilot, licensing, exclusivity, acquisition, IP assignment, data transfer, or money movement becomes real without qualified legal/CPA support.
Pilot should pause or decline if buyer expects a free pilot that transfers substantial value or protected method knowledge.

purpose: Create buyer-safe pilot success/failure criteria that measure pilot usefulness without claiming production validation or automated verdict capability.

success_criteria.description: Buyer can provide observable exported traces without protected internals.
success_criteria.measurement: Trace export includes visible sequence, event/state fields, claimed final state if available, field dictionary, and scope memo.

success_criteria.description: Buyer retains labels or can perform human adjudication.
success_criteria.measurement: Buyer confirms label ownership and final adjudication process.

success_criteria.description: Candidate-discrepancy memos are useful to human reviewers.
success_criteria.measurement: Buyer reviewer can classify memo prompts as useful, ambiguous, insufficient, or not useful.

success_criteria.description: Pilot stays observable-only and avoids overclaiming.
success_criteria.measurement: No model weights, source code, private prompts, hidden activations, private chain-of-thought, automated verdicts, production validation, or compliance claims are used.

success_criteria.description: SpecShift can produce a bounded delivery packet consistently.
success_criteria.measurement: Intake, provenance, memo, limitations, and delivery checklist are completed.
